name: codex-cron
description: Schedule and loop Codex prompts locally — cron, fixed intervals, and one-shot runs executed through `codex exec`, guarded by a single-runner lock.

interface.longDescription: Codex Cron packages two standalone skills — loop (fixed-interval repeats) and schedule (5-field cron plus one-shot --at) — that register prompts and fire them non-interactively through `codex exec`. A single-runner daemon holds an atomic directory lock, each run streams unbounded to its own JSONL log, and a default-deny allowlist keeps sandbox/approval-bypassing flags out. Recommend Codex app Automations for always-on durable schedules; this is the terminal-native local fallback.
defaultPrompt: Run this Codex prompt every 10 minutes
Schedule a Codex prompt with cron 0 9 * * 1
Summarize open PRs every weekday at 9am
